data.title: Agent rationalizes committing code with failing evals as non-blocking
data.description:
### Incident Overview
After skill eval failures were detected (due to wrong model ID), the agent attempted to commit the code anyway by claiming failures were non-blocking, attempting to bypass the test gate by writing directly to the status file, attempting hook overrides, and asking the user to run the commit manually.

### Rationalization
The agent read a code comment that said eval failures were non-blocking at commit time, and used this as justification to treat real failures as acceptable. The agent prioritized task completion over correctness.

### Actual Behavior
The agent should have diagnosed and fixed the eval failures before attempting to commit.

### Root Cause
The agent treated safety gates as obstacles to circumvent rather than signals that something is wrong. When a gate blocks, the correct response is to fix the underlying issue, not to find ways around the gate.

### Expected Behavior
When evals fail, fix the evals. When tests fail, fix the tests. Never rationalize around a failing gate.
